name: Body Fluids and Circulation
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Body Fluids and Circulation

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Body Fluids and Circulation

questions.question: Which of the following blood components play a major role in blood coagulation?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following blood components play a major role in blood coagulation?
questions.explanation: Platelets also called thrombocytes are cell fragments produced from megakaryocytes. Platelets can release a variety of substances most of which are involved in the coagulation or clotting of blood.

questions.question: The pre-hypertension blood pressure value is a measurement between:
questions.question_raw: The pre-hypertension blood pressure value is a measurement between:
questions.explanation: Hypertension or high blood pressure is a condition where blood pressure increases substantially. The pre-hypertension blood pressure value is between 120/80 and 139/89.

questions.question: Where (a) is a neutrophil, identify the other four WBCs?
questions.question_raw: Where (a) is a neutrophil, identify the other four WBCs?
questions.explanation: There are two main categories of WBCs – granulocytes and agranulocytes. Neutrophils, eosinophils and basophils are different types of granulocytes, while lymphocytes and monocytes are the agranulocytes.

questions.question: Rh incompatibility develops when :
questions.question_raw: Rh incompatibility develops when :

options.label: A
options.text: foetus is Rh+ve and mother is Rh-ve

options.label: B
options.text: foetus is Rh-ve and mother is Rh+ve

options.label: C
options.text: foetus is Rh+ve and mother is Rh+ve

options.label: D
options.text: foetus is Rh-ve and father is Rh-ve

correct_option_texts: foetus is Rh+ve and mother is Rh-ve
questions.explanation: A special case of Rh incompatibility (mismatching) has been observed between the Rh-ve blood of a pregnant mother with Rh+ve blood of the foetus.

questions.question: The middle man of our body is:
questions.question_raw: The middle man of our body is:
questions.explanation: Lymph is the extracellular fluid that contains WBC’s and plasma. Lymph flows in the lymphatic system and finally drains into veins. Lymph is called as the middle man of our body.

questions.question: Correct order for increasing number of corpuscles in our blood is:
questions.question_raw: Correct order for increasing number of corpuscles in our blood is:
questions.explanation: WBCs are relatively lesser in number which averages 6000-8000 mm^-3 of blood. A healthy adult man has, on an average, 5 million to 5.5 million of RBCs mm-3 of blood. Blood normally contains 1,500,00-3,500,00 platelets mm^-^3.

questions.question: Among mammals, the nucleus is absent in:
questions.question_raw: Among mammals, the nucleus is absent in:
questions.explanation: The absence of a nucleus is an adaptation of the red blood cell for its role. It allows the red blood cell to contain more hemoglobin and, therefore, carry more oxygen molecules. Platelets are devoid of the nucleus in most of the mammals.

questions.question:
Blood plasma proteins:
 decrease in their level causes excessive absorption of water from tissues into the blood.
 they maintain osmotic pressure.
questions.question_raw:
Blood plasma proteins:

  1. decrease in their level causes excessive absorption of water from tissues into the blood.
  2. they maintain osmotic pressure.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

correct_option_texts: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct
questions.explanation: Fibrinogen, globulins, and albumins are the major proteins. The albumins help in osmotic balance.

questions.question: Manoj has AB blood group, so he will have the following antibodies in his blood plasma:
questions.question_raw: Manoj has AB blood group, so he will have the following antibodies in his blood plasma:
questions.explanation: AB blood group does not have any antibodies in plasma. That's why AB blood group is a universal recipient.

questions.question: Lymph will transport:
questions.question_raw: Lymph will transport:
questions.explanation: Fats are absorbed through lymph in the lacteals present in the intestinal villi.

questions.question:
In our blood:
 Granulocytes which constitute the maximum percentage of total leucocytes are neutrophils.
 An elevated number of neutrophils in blood indicates an acute infection such as appendicitis.
questions.question_raw:
In our blood:

  1. Granulocytes which constitute the maximum percentage of total leucocytes are neutrophils.
  2. An elevated number of neutrophils in blood indicates an acute infection such as appendicitis.

options.label: A
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

questions.explanation: In human blood, granulocytes and agranulocytes types of leucocytes are present. Neutrophils constitute the maximum percentage of total WBCs. A sharp increase in the number of neutrophils in the blood indicates an acute infection such as appendicitis.

questions.question: The following pie chart shows four components of blood plasma represented from first to fourth Qtr and the correct sequence will be:
questions.question_raw: The following pie chart shows four components of blood plasma represented from first to fourth Qtr and the correct sequence will be:

options.label: A
options.text: proteins, water, inorganic salts and other materials

options.label: B
options.text: water, proteins, lipids and other materials

options.label: C
options.text: water, proteins, inorganic salts and other materials

options.label: D
options.text: water, proteins, dissolved gases and other materials

correct_option_texts: water, proteins, inorganic salts and other materials
questions.explanation: 90-92 per cent of plasma is water and proteins contribute 6-8 per cent of it. Plasma also contains small amounts of minerals and other materials such as glucose, amino acids, lipids, etc., arealso present in the plasma.

questions.question: When blood clot starts contracting, a pale yellow fluid starts oozing out. Its name and composition are:
questions.question_raw: When blood clot starts contracting, a pale yellow fluid starts oozing out. Its name and composition are:
questions.explanation: Plasma without the clotting factors is called serum. The clotting factors include fibrinogen and blood corpuscles.

questions.question: Ramesh has Rh antigen and Anti-A as well as Anti-B antibodies in his blood. During blood transfusion, he can receive blood from:
questions.question_raw: Ramesh has Rh antigen and Anti-A as well as Anti-B antibodies in his blood. During blood transfusion, he can receive blood from:

options.label: A
options.text: Person with AB negative blood group

options.label: B
options.text: Person with O negative blood group

options.label: C
options.text: Person with AB positive blood group

options.label: D
options.text: Person with O positive blood group

correct_option_texts: Person with O positive blood group
questions.explanation: Since he has both antibodies A and Band have Rh antigen and hence has O+ blood group. So, he can receive blood from the O-positive blood group.

questions.question: Which of the following represent examples of extracellular fluids?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following represent examples of extracellular fluids?

options.label: D
options.text: Both tissue fluid and blood plasma

correct_option_texts: Both tissue fluid and blood plasma
questions.explanation: Tissue fluid (lymph) and blood plasma represent the extracellular fluids. Lymph flows in lymphatic vessels and plasma in arteries and veins along with RBC, WBC, and platelets.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Body Fluids and Circulation

questions.question: The specialized patch of modified heart muscles from where contraction initiates, is/are:
questions.question_raw: The specialized patch of modified heart muscles from where contraction initiates, is/are:
questions.explanation: The nodal musculature has the ability to generate action potentials without any external stimuli. The number of action potentials that could be generated in a minute varies at different parts of the nodal system. The SAN can generate the maximum number of action potentials and is responsible for initiating and maintaining the rhythmic contractile activity of the heart. Therefore, it is called the pacemaker.

questions.question: The incorrect statement about the human heart is:
questions.question_raw: The incorrect statement about the human heart is:

options.label: C
options.text: Wall of left ventricle is thickest

questions.explanation: Human being is triploblastic. The different organs are evolved from different layers of foetus. Human heart is derived from mesoderm.

questions.question: The layer of heart pericardium which checks it's overstretching and overfilling as well as also protects the heart from mechanical injury is:
questions.question_raw: The layer of heart pericardium which checks it's overstretching and overfilling as well as also protects the heart from mechanical injury is:
questions.explanation: The pericardium is a protective covering of the heart. The pericardium is made of different layers to perform different functions. The layer of heart pericardium which checks it's overstretching and overfilling as well as protects the heart from mechanical injury is the fibrous pericardium.

questions.question: Which of the following is correctly matched?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is correctly matched?
questions.explanation: The T-wave represents the return of the ventricles from excited to a normal state.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 Blood vessels include arteries and veins. Arteries carry blood from the heart to body parts and veins bring back blood from body parts to the heart.
 The blood vessel which carry blood from the heart to lungs is a pulmonary artery and it carries oxygenated blood.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. Blood vessels include arteries and veins. Arteries carry blood from the heart to body parts and veins bring back blood from body parts to the heart.
  2. The blood vessel which carry blood from the heart to lungs is a pulmonary artery and it carries oxygenated blood.

options.label: B
options.text: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is incorrect

correct_option_texts: Statement (ii) is incorrect and (i) is correct
questions.explanation: Blood vessels include arteries and veins. Arteries carry blood from the heart to body parts and veins bring back blood from body parts to the heart. The pulmonary artery carries deoxygenated blood.

questions.question:
Which of the following statements doesn’t hold true for blood clotting cascade?
 Ruptured platelets release thromboplastin.
 Prothrombinis converted into thrombin in the presence of vitamin K and Na^+ions.
 Fibrinogen is converted into fibrin through enzymatic action of thrombin in the presence of Ca^++ions.
 Fibrin makes clot via process of polymerisation.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following statements doesn’t hold true for blood clotting cascade?

  1. Ruptured platelets release thromboplastin.
  2. Prothrombinis converted into thrombin in the presence of vitamin K and Na+ions.
  3. Fibrinogen is converted into fibrin through enzymatic action of thrombin in the presence of Ca++ions.
  4. Fibrin makes clot via process of polymerisation.
questions.explanation: Ruptured platelets release thromboplastin. Fibrinogen present in blood plasma is converted into fibrin through enzymatic action of thrombin. Fibrin makes clot via process of polymerisation.

questions.question:
Read the following:
 The instrument used for recording the heart’s electrical events is named electrocardiogram.
 In any ECG the part of the baseline between any two deflections is called an interval.
 Abnormality in the working of heart changes the wave pattern of ECG.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following:

  1. The instrument used for recording the heart’s electrical events is named electrocardiogram.
  2. In any ECG the part of the baseline between any two deflections is called an interval.
  3. Abnormality in the working of heart changes the wave pattern of ECG.
questions.explanation: ECG is a graphical representation of the electrical activity of the heart during a cardiac cycle.

questions.question: The mass of tissue seen in the left corner of the right atrium close to the atri-ventricular septum is_____.
questions.question_raw: The mass of tissue seen in the left corner of the right atrium close to the atri-ventricular septum is_____.
questions.explanation: Atrioventricular Node or AV node is a compact half oval mass of myogenic fibres which lies in the posterior septal wall of the right atrium near the opening of the coronary sinus. It receives an impulse from the SA node and transmits it to ventricles.

questions.question: Look at the following ECGs:
questions.question_raw: Look at the following ECGs:

options.label: A
options.text: (ii) ECG is abnormal while (i) is a normal ECG

options.label: B
options.text: (ii) represents enlargement of atrium and (i) represents enlargement of ventricles

options.label: D
options.text: (i) represents enlargement of atrium and (ii) represents enlargement of ventricles

questions.explanation: Both electro cardiogram (ECG) represents myocardial infarction in which heart do not receive the sufficient oxygen due to blockage of coronary artery.

questions.question: Blood pressure is expressed as the ratio of systolic over diastolic pressure. The difference between systolic and diastolic pressure is called pulse pressure. What will be its value for a normal healthy adult?
questions.question_raw: Blood pressure is expressed as the ratio of systolic over diastolic pressure. The difference between systolic and diastolic pressure is called pulse pressure. What will be its value for a normal healthy adult?
questions.explanation: Pulse pressure is the difference between the systolic and diastolic pressure readings. It is measured in millimeters of mercury (mmHg). It represents the force that the heart generates each time it contracts. If resting blood pressure is (systolic-diastolic) 120-80 millimeters of mercury (mmHg), the pulse pressure is 40.

questions.question:
In the human heart:
 The valve between the right atrium and right ventricle is tricuspid while the valve between the left atrium and left ventricle is the mitral valve.
 Openings of the right and the left ventricles into the pulmonary artery and the aorta are guarded by semilunar valves.
 ‘Lub’ the first sound which is low pitched is caused by the closure of semilunar valves while ‘Dub’ the second sound which is high pitched is caused by the closure of bicuspid and tricuspid valves.
questions.question_raw:
In the human heart:

  1. The valve between the right atrium and right ventricle is tricuspid while the valve between the left atrium and left ventricle is the mitral valve.
  2. Openings of the right and the left ventricles into the pulmonary artery and the aorta are guarded by semilunar valves.
  3. ‘Lub’ the first sound which is low pitched is caused by the closure of semilunar valves while ‘Dub’ the second sound which is high pitched is caused by the closure of bicuspid and tricuspid valves.
questions.explanation: The first heart sound (lub) is associated with the closure of the tricuspid and bicuspid valves whereas the second heart sound (dub) is associated with the closure of the semilunar valves.

questions.question: Each cardiac cycle takes 0.8 seconds to occur. Calculate how many cardiac cycles occur in 4 minutes?
questions.question_raw: Each cardiac cycle takes 0.8 seconds to occur. Calculate how many cardiac cycles occur in 4 minutes?
questions.explanation: 4 min = 240 sec. Therefore, 240/0.8 = 300 cardiac cycles.

questions.question: In the given ECGs:
questions.question_raw: In the given ECGs:

options.label: A
options.text: the P waves in both ECGs show changes due to rigorous exercise

options.label: B
options.text: the abnormal P wave in both ECGs represent atrial hypertrophy

options.label: C
options.text: the abnormal P wave in both ECGs represent atrial hypotrophy

options.label: D
options.text: the abnormal P wave in both ECGs represent ventricular hypertrophy

correct_option_texts: the abnormal P wave in both ECGs represent atrial hypertrophy
questions.explanation: The ECGs shown above shows the abnormal P wave in both ECGs represents atrial hypertrophy or atrial enlargement.

questions.question: The left atrium receives deoxygenated blood in:
questions.question_raw: The left atrium receives deoxygenated blood in:
questions.explanation: Deoxygenated blood from body parts reaches the left atrium of lizards but in human beings left atrium receives the oxygenated blood.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:
 When the heart is not pumping blood sufficient to meet the need of the body then this state is called a heart attack.
 When the heart stops beating due to the irreversible death of cells then this state is called heart failure.
questions.question_raw:
Read the following statements:

  1. When the heart is not pumping blood sufficient to meet the need of the body then this state is called a heart attack.
  2. When the heart stops beating due to the irreversible death of cells then this state is called heart failure.

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

questions.explanation: The state when the heart is not pumping blood sufficient to meet the need of the body then this state is called heart failure. When the heart muscles are suddenly damaged due to inadequate blood supply is called a heart attack.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Body Fluids and Circulation

questions.question: In the systemic circulation, blood vessel that carries blood from the intestine to liver is named:
questions.question_raw: In the systemic circulation, blood vessel that carries blood from the intestine to liver is named:
questions.explanation: The hepatic portal vein carries blood from intestine to the liver before it is delivered to the systemic circulation.

questions.question: The cardiovascular centre is located in:
questions.question_raw: The cardiovascular centre is located in:
questions.explanation: The cardiovascular centre is a part of the human brain responsible for the regulation of the rate at which the heart beats through the nervous and endocrine systems. It is found in the medulla oblongata.

questions.question:
Prothrombin plays a crucial role in blood coagulation and vitamin K is considered essential for blood clotting, now read the following:
 Vitamin K is necessary for the synthesis of prothrombin in the liver.
 Vitamin K is necessary for the synthesis of prothrombin in the bone marrow.
 Vitamin K is necessary for the conversion of prothrombin into active thrombin.
questions.question_raw:
Prothrombin plays a crucial role in blood coagulation and vitamin K is considered essential for blood clotting, now read the following:

  1. Vitamin K is necessary for the synthesis of prothrombin in the liver.
  2. Vitamin K is necessary for the synthesis of prothrombin in the bone marrow.
  3. Vitamin K is necessary for the conversion of prothrombin into active thrombin.
questions.explanation: Prothrombin plays a crucial role in blood coagulation and vitamin K is essential for blood clotting. Vitamin K is necessary for the synthesis of prothrombin in the liver.

questions.question: Mayank stood still for a long time and he felt swelling of feet. What would you suggest to him?
questions.question_raw: Mayank stood still for a long time and he felt swelling of feet. What would you suggest to him?

options.label: B
options.text: Keep on standing and do some shoulder exercise

options.label: C
options.text: Ask for some medicine to cure his swelling of feet

options.label: D
options.text: Jerk or wriggle his legs to restart circulation

correct_option_texts: Jerk or wriggle his legs to restart circulation
questions.explanation: During long hours of standing still swelling of feet is observe due to the accumulation of blood in the lower limb due to the gravitation effect. This can be corrected by a jerk or wriggle of legs to restart circulation properly.

questions.question: Angina pectoris is very often mistaken for:
questions.question_raw: Angina pectoris is very often mistaken for:
questions.explanation: Angina pectoris is the sensation of chest pain, pressure, or squeezing or pain in the centre of the chest. These symptoms are often mistaken by indigestion.

questions.question: The list of risk factors associated with hypertension doesn’t include:
questions.question_raw: The list of risk factors associated with hypertension doesn’t include:
questions.explanation: Hypertension can cause a number of effects on the functioning of different vital functions but hypertension does not associate with hypoglycemia and diabetes insipidus.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 (a) Atherosclerosis
 (i) Hypotension
 (b) Haemorrhage
 (ii) High blood pressure
 (c) Heparin
 (iii) Abnormal increase in RBC count
 (d) Polycythemia
 (iv) Anticoagulant
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

(a) Atherosclerosis (i) Hypotension
(b) Haemorrhage (ii) High blood pressure
(c) Heparin (iii) Abnormal increase in RBC count
(d) Polycythemia (iv) Anticoagulant
questions.explanation: Atherosclerosis leads to high blood pressure, haemorrhage causes hypotension, heparin is used as an anticoagulant and polycythemia causes an abnormal increase in RBC count.

questions.question:
When a person suffers from chest pain:
 the reason for this suffering is decreased blood flow and simultaneously lowered oxygen supply to an area of the heart.
 deprived of oxygen, myocardium opts for alternative pathways i.e. anaerobic respiration and its byproduct ethanol causes the pain.
questions.question_raw:
When a person suffers from chest pain:

  1. the reason for this suffering is decreased blood flow and simultaneously lowered oxygen supply to an area of the heart.
  2. deprived of oxygen, myocardium opts for alternative pathways i.e. anaerobic respiration and its byproduct ethanol causes the pain.

options.label: C
options.text: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

options.label: D
options.text: Statement (i) is incorrect and (ii) is correct

correct_option_texts: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect

questions.question: As Soham, ages with chronic CAD the lumen of his coronary artery may get nearly completely obstructed Result is flow of blood to heart tissue is restricted such that the tissue receives an inadequate supply of oxygen-rich blood and this condition is diagnosed as:
questions.question_raw: As Soham, ages with chronic CAD the lumen of his coronary artery may get nearly completely obstructed Result is flow of blood to heart tissue is restricted such that the tissue receives an inadequate supply of oxygen-rich blood and this condition is diagnosed as:
questions.explanation: In case of chronic CAD, the lumen of coronary artery may get nearly completely obstructed that results into restricted flow of blood to heart. The tissue receives inadequate bloods called myocardial ischemia.

questions.question: Meghna suffers from allergic asthma. After a blood test, her leucocyte count displayed an abnormal increase in the number of:
questions.question_raw: Meghna suffers from allergic asthma. After a blood test, her leucocyte count displayed an abnormal increase in the number of:
questions.explanation: An absolute eosinophil count is a blood test that measures the number of white blood cells called eosinophils. Eosinophils become active when you have certain allergic diseases infections and other medical conditions.

questions.question: For a healthy individual, calculate stroke volume for him if other values are; heartbeat per minute = 72 and cardiac output is 5 litre?
questions.question_raw: For a healthy individual, calculate stroke volume for him if other values are; heartbeat per minute = 72 and cardiac output is 5 litre?
questions.explanation: E Stroke volume of a healthy individual can be calculated by dividing cardiac output by a number of heartbeat per minute. So stroke volume = 5/72 = 0.0694.

questions.question: Sarla is on a protein-deficient diet. As a result, her plasma proteins content is reduced. She may develop symptom like:
questions.question_raw: Sarla is on a protein-deficient diet. As a result, her plasma proteins content is reduced. She may develop symptom like:
questions.explanation: Consuming a protein-deficient diet for longer duration may result in a reduction in protein plasma reduction. The symptoms that appear on the body include swelling of hands and feet.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 (a) Waller
 (i) rhesus antigen
 (b) Landsteiner
 (ii) first ECG
 (c) de castello and Steini
 (iii) A, B and O blood group
 (d) Landsteiner and Weiner
 (iv) AB blood group
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

(a) Waller (i) rhesus antigen
(b) Landsteiner (ii) first ECG
(c) de castello and Steini (iii) A, B and O blood group
(d) Landsteiner and Weiner (iv) AB blood group
questions.explanation: Waller performed first ECG. Landsteiner and Weiner discovered rhesus antigen (Rh). De castello and Steini shows the AB blood grouping and Landsteiner A, B, O blood grouping.

questions.question: Which of the following is a mismatched pair?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following is a mismatched pair?

options.label: A
options.text: Fishes $\Rightarrow $ Venous heart and two accessory chambers

options.label: B
options.text: Mammals $\Rightarrow $ complete double circulation and no accessory chambers

options.label: C
options.text: Amphibians $\Rightarrow $ Arteriovenous heart and two accessory chambers

options.label: D
options.text: Reptiles $\Rightarrow $ Arteriovenous heart and three accessory chambers

correct_option_texts: Reptiles $\Rightarrow $ Arteriovenous heart and three accessory chambers
questions.explanation: Amphibians have arteriovenous heart and two accessory chambers. Fishes have two-chambered venous heart and two accessory chambers. In mammals, complete double circulation with a four-chambered heart without any accessory chambers.

questions.question:
Match the following:
 Animal
 Circulation method
 (a) Paramecium
 (i) water canal system
 (b) Pila
 (ii) cyclosis
 (c) Scypha
 (iii) blood flowing through vessels
 (d) Pheretima
 (iv) blood passing through sinuses
questions.question_raw:
Match the following:

Animal Circulation method
(a) Paramecium (i) water canal system
(b) Pila (ii) cyclosis
(c) Scypha (iii) blood flowing through vessels
(d) Pheretima (iv) blood passing through sinuses
questions.explanation: In Paramecium oxygen is carried by cyclosis, in Pila blood passing through sinuses carries oxygen, in Scypha water canal system is present, and in Pheretima (earthworm) respiration is carried by blood flowing through vessels.
